finding_results.notes: Specified what the Meridian patch is meant to do, what it has actually been tested on, what acute post-install costs are known, what Yusuf and Petra cannot certify, and why earlier monitored installs still look rational despite limited competition-load evidence.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_08.md:161; chapters/chapter_08.md:163; chapters/chapter_08.md:171; chapters/chapter_08.md:175; chapters/chapter_08.md:177; chapters/chapter_08.md:189; chapters/chapter_08.md:199; chapters/chapter_08.md:203
finding_results.notes: Compressed the repeated anonymous-signing run after Tomas's signature into one representative beat, then moved directly to the completed-agreements leverage, Lena's unsigned choice, appointment scheduling, and Kai's closing refusal beat.

Three targeted changes roughen the crowd exchange. (1) Line 71: added overlap where someone lost in the binder asks 'Which section is that even in?' while the pointed question is still being pressed — partial misunderstanding amid crowd pressure. (2) Line 103: Adewale's question revised from a single polished rhetorical line to a self-correcting read-through of the clause ('This part — survives retirement. So when I'm done, when I'm forty-five coaching kids somewhere, they still have all of this?'). (3) Line 111: the gymnast's three-beat thesis ('That's not bars. That's me before bars. That's my blood.') compressed to one interrupted utterance ('that's not even — that's my blood'). The crowd now has two clear instances of roughness (overlap/partial misunderstanding at line 71, self-correction at line 103), and no more than one non-core athlete (Aissatou, whose confrontational question is already alive) delivers a clean multi-sentence issue summary. Yusuf and Petra's technical-distinction dialogue preserved unchanged.

pass_summaries.summary: Revised the chapter so the patch decision now reads as a concrete tradeoff between lethal blackout risk and a bench-tested, supervised install with known short-term costs and unresolved race-load uncertainty. The back third now reaches institutional escalation faster after Tomas signs, preserving the economic trap, Lena's blank line, and Kai's refusal without re-proving the herd turn through multiple near-duplicate signers.

Roughened the contract-debate crowd exchange across three points: added binder confusion as overlap in the cyclist section, gave Adewale a self-correcting clause-reading instead of a polished rhetorical question, and compressed the gymnast's three-beat diagnosis into one interrupted line. The room now sounds more like a frightened group working through a contract than a panel handing thematic clauses from one spokesperson to the next. Core informational beats, Yusuf/Petra technical distinction, Kai interiority, and chapter architecture preserved.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=3, PARTIAL=0, UNRESOLVED=0).
